description: Exposing ipcRenderer, require, or a generic channel-invoke function through preload, letting the page reach any main-process handler.
body: Failure mode: the preload uses contextBridge to expose ipcRenderer directly or an invoke(channel, args) pass-through, so a compromised renderer can call any IPC channel and trigger privileged operations it should never reach. Detection signals: window.api exposes send/invoke with arbitrary channels; the page can require modules; the preload surface is large and generic. Do not expose raw IPC or modules. Safer fallback: expose only named, purpose-specific functions that each call a fixed channel, keep the surface small and auditable, and validate every call in the main process.
